source.notes: Optimus __ Flock demo - 2026_06_30 09_29 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: Max Riemer of Optimus ran a live demo of a freight-broker prospecting and shipper-intelligence platform for Michael Munday of Flock Freight, with Michael driving most of the session (68% of words) by stress-testing the tool against his own live accounts like Aldi and Dollar General. Michael found the bill-of-lading-sourced data and Highway capacity-matching genuinely compelling but flagged significant UI/contact-prioritization issues and a central concern that the tool is too broad ('jack of all trades but a master of none') for a single buying group. They agreed loosely to reconnect after the holiday once Michael consults colleagues, but no dated next step was locked.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Max was clearly unscripted and responsive — he tracked Michael's on-screen exploration, guided navigation in real time, and captured feedback for action rather than reciting a pitch, though he leaned on 'I'll ask our CTO' several times, showing gaps in his own product knowledge.

evidence.quote: No, I we need to hear it all and this will be recorded. I'll just send it over to Toby, our CTO, and he'll probably have it fixed within an hour.
are your carrier sales reps responsible for the growth within shippers?

value_prop_clarity.explanation: Concrete, differentiated claims surfaced — data sourced from scanned bills of lading (not scraped), 30-day contact refresh, and Highway-integrated capacity-to-shipper matching — enough for a neutral listener to summarize it as a freight-specific ZoomInfo built on real shipment paperwork.

evidence.quote: this isn't scraped, right? So the customers we see are from paperwork
the contacts get updated every 30 days
source shippers that match up well there. That's sort of the AI portion of it

relevance.explanation: The pitch was tightly tailored to Flock as a brokerage — Max anchored on Michael's Highway TMS, his carrier-capacity/gap-filling use case, and ran the entire demo on Michael's real accounts and lanes.

evidence.quote: source shippers that match up well there. That's sort of the AI portion of it
are your carrier sales reps responsible for the growth within shippers?

discovery_progression.explanation: Max asked a few sharp qualifying questions but never proposed a concrete, dated next step — he let the buyer define a vague 'after the holiday' reconnect and closed passively rather than owning a scheduled follow-up.

evidence.quote: after the holiday, let's try and connect and see where things stand
Let me up with any questions you got.

Max Riemer.coaching_action: Close every demo by proposing a specific date and time for the next meeting and securing verbal agreement on the call — don't let a warm buyer leave with only a self-defined 'after the holiday' follow-up you don't own.

signals.signal: Repeated unprompted enthusiasm for the core data
signals.quote: this is super compelling to me

signals.signal: Buyer-owned next steps in his own words (keep testing, loop in Corey, reconnect)
signals.quote: after the holiday, let's try and connect and see where things stand

signals.signal: High question depth — stress-tested the tool against his own live accounts and pushed on data provenance
signals.quote: Can I see that raw data or no?

signals.signal: Substantive fit/adoption objection that tempers the interest
signals.quote: you're setting it up for it's a jack of all trades but a master of none
